message.content:
- Silicon Valley Bank suffered a run on deposits, prompting regulators to devise a rescue plan over the weekend.
- The collapse of SVB has highlighted an underappreciated risk within the system: unrecognised losses from long-term bonds.
- Across America's banking system, these unrecognised losses are vast: $620bn at the end of 2022, equivalent to about a third of the combined capital cushions of America’s banks.
- Rising interest rates have left the system vulnerable and investors have taken fright, with $229bn wiped off the market value of America's banks this month.
- Regulators must use the year ahead to make the system safer by removing exemptions that apply to mid-sized banks and building a regime that recognizes the risks from rising interest rates.
